# Lliam-GOV Control Matrix

**Document status:** Phase 2 deliverable, 2026-05-25. Pending Jerome's written sign-off per Rev. 3 plan §9 Phase 2 exit.
**Source of truth:** `evidence/control-matrix.csv` (machine-readable, regenerated by `evidence/phase2/build-matrix.py`).
**Scope:** Lliam-GOV-touching controls from CMMC L2 / NIST SP 800-171 Rev. 2, ISO/IEC 42001:2023, and ISO/IEC 27001:2022 (per Rev. 3 plan §3).

## How to read this matrix

Each row in `evidence/control-matrix.csv` carries:

| Column | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `control_id` | Standard-qualified identifier (e.g., `SP800-171_3.13.16`, `ISO42001_A.4.3`) |
| `standard` | The publishing standard's name and version |
| `statement_verbatim` | The published text, extracted from the source PDF via `pdftotext -layout` |
| `source_pdf` | The filename of the source PDF in `/Volumes/WORKSPACE/3-Resources/` |
| `rev3_equivalent_id` | (SP 800-171 only) The Rev. 3 control ID that supersedes this Rev. 2 control |
| `rev3_statement_verbatim` | (SP 800-171 only) The Rev. 3 verbatim text for the equivalent control |
| `lliam_owner` | The §5 implementation module + function/path that satisfies the control |
| `evidence_artifact` | The evidence-tree path where operating evidence will be captured (Phase 5) |
| `cross_mappings` | Other standards' controls this row also satisfies |
| `current_state` | Implementation state: `not_implemented`, `scaffolded`, `implemented`, `tested`, `evidence_captured` |
| `notes` | Per-row commentary, including any ID corrections vs the Rev. 3 plan |

## Extraction methodology

The matrix was built by running `pdftotext -layout` against four local source PDFs in `/Volumes/WORKSPACE/3-Resources/` and applying per-row regex extractors to capture the requirement statement + DISCUSSION/CONTROL text for each control. Zero NotebookLM round-trips: the script in `evidence/phase2/build-matrix.py` is fully reproducible from the local file system. Pivot rationale documented in the script header.

Source PDFs (with SHA-256 to lock the version):

```
$ shasum -a 256 *.pdf
NIST_SP_800-171_r2.pdf
NIST_SP_800-171_r3.pdf
ISO_42001-2023.pdf
ISO 27001-2022.pdf
```

## Critical caveats

### SP 800-171 Rev. 2 was withdrawn May 2024

NIST formally **withdrew SP 800-171 Rev. 2 on May 14, 2024** and superseded it with Rev. 3. CMMC Level 2 still references Rev. 2 (DoD has not yet formally transitioned the CMMC L2 control set to Rev. 3 as of the build date). Lliam-GOV's bar is therefore Rev. 2 by transitive obligation through CMMC L2 — but auditors reviewing this matrix should be aware that:

1. Citations in this matrix point at withdrawn-but-still-CMMC-canonical Rev. 2 text.
2. A future CMMC update may transition the L2 baseline to Rev. 3. When that happens, the affected rows in this matrix must be re-verified against Rev. 3 published text.
3. The Rev. 3 plan §3.3 explicitly names "SP 800-171 Rev. 2" as the bar — this matrix honors that.
4. To future-proof the matrix, each SP 800-171 row carries a `rev3_equivalent_id` column with the Rev. 3 renumbered control + verbatim text. **The most consequential Rev. 3 change for Lliam-GOV: `3.13.16` (Protect CUI at rest) was withdrawn and incorporated into `03.13.08` (cryptographic protection).** Phase 3's `key_manager.py` satisfies both.

### ISO 42001 Annex A — plan §3.4 IDs corrected in Phase 2

The Rev. 3 plan §3.4 ended with "Annex A — to confirm in Phase 2" and listed six guessed control IDs. Re-verification against the actual published ISO/IEC 42001:2023 surfaced that **4 of 6 IDs were wrong**. Corrected mappings:

| Plan §3.4 said | Plan's description | Actual ISO/IEC 42001:2023 |
|---|---|---|
| A.6.2.4 | "Concepts" | A.6.2.4 is **"AI system verification and validation"** (kept, reinterpreted) |
| A.7.4 | "Quality of system and data" | A.7.4 is **"Quality of data for AI systems"** (kept, minor title diff) |
| A.8.2 | "AI system impact assessment" | Actually **A.5.2** "AI system impact assessment" |
| A.8.4 | "Data governance" | A.8.4 is **"Communication of incidents"**. Data governance lives at **A.4.3 "Data resources"** |
| A.9.2 | "Objectives for responsible development" | Actually **A.6.1.2** "Objectives for responsible development of AI systems" |
| A.10.2 | "Allocation of responsibilities" | Actually **A.3.2** "AI roles and responsibilities" |

The plan author was likely confusing Annex A numbering (`A.X.Y.Z` topic-grouped) with body-clause numbering (clauses 4–10, ISO management-system convention). The matrix uses the corrected IDs.

Phase 2 also added **two Annex A controls not in plan §3.4** that are directly relevant to Lliam-GOV's hardening overlay:

- **A.6.2.6 "AI system operation and monitoring"** — covers the runtime monitoring posture
- **A.6.2.8 "AI system recording of event logs"** — direct AIMS counterpart to the audit log

## Counts

| Standard | Rows |
|---|---:|
| SP 800-171 Rev. 2 (agent-touching subset, with Rev. 3 cross-mapping) | 18 |
| ISO/IEC 42001:2023 — Annex A controls | 8 (6 from plan + 2 added in Phase 2) |
| ISO/IEC 42001:2023 — body clauses (4, 6, 7.5, 8, 9.1, 10) | 6 |
| ISO/IEC 27001:2022 — Annex A controls | 23 |
| **Total** | **55** |

## Lliam-GOV §5 implementation owners (quick reference)

| Module / path | Plan §5 anchor | Phase that lands it |
|---|---|---|
| `lliam_gov/security/key_manager.py` | §5.1 — AES-256-GCM, scrypt-derived, Keychain-backed | Phase 3 |
| `lliam_gov/security/audit_logger.py` | §5.2 — JSONL hash-chained audit, monthly rotation, AEP export | Phase 3 |
| `lliam_gov/security/principal.py` | §5.3 — OS-uid binding, refuses root | Phase 4 |
| `lliam_gov/security/capabilities.py` | §5.3 — capability-tagged tool dispatch | Phase 4 |
| `lliam_gov/security/egress.py` | §5.4 — egress allowlist + TLS 1.2+ enforced | Phase 4 |
| `lliam_gov/security/runtime_guard.py` | §5.5 — workspace mode 0700, umask 0077, FIPS-OpenSSL hard gate | Phase 4 |
| `lliam_gov/security/selfmod_gate.py` | §5.5.bis — proposal/approval gate over dynamic self-modification | Phase 4 |
| `lliam_gov/security/cui.py` | §5.6 — CUI marking + audit only (governance-controlled enforcement) | Phase 4 |
| `gateway/platforms/{slack,email,telegram}.py` | §6.5 — narrowed gateway surface | Phase 1 ✓ |
| `hermes_cli/web_server.py` + `hermes_cli/main.py` | §6.5 — dashboard loopback-only, break-glass removed | Phase 1 ✓ |
| `evidence/sbom/` + `hermes_cli/security_advisories.py` | §5.7 — CycloneDX SBOM + quarterly SCA cadence | Phase 5 |

## Section: SP 800-171 Rev. 2 (CMMC L2 baseline) — 18 controls

| Control | Title | Lliam owner | Rev. 3 equivalent | Cross-mapped |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 3.1.1 | Limit system access | `principal.py` + gateway allowlists | 03.01.01 | ISO 27001 A.8.5; ISO 42001 A.3.2 |
| 3.1.2 | Limit transactions/functions to authorized | `capabilities.py` | 03.01.02 | ISO 27001 A.8.5 |
| 3.1.13 | Cryptographic mechanisms — remote-access conf. | `egress.py` | 03.01.13 **(Withdrawn — moved to SC family in Rev. 3)** | ISO 27001 A.8.24 |
| 3.1.20 | Verify/control external connections | `egress.py` | 03.01.20 | ISO 27001 A.8.20–A.8.23 |
| 3.3.1 | Create/retain audit records | `audit_logger.py` | 03.03.01 | ISO 27001 A.8.15; ISO 42001 Clause 7.5/9.1 |
| 3.3.2 | Uniquely traceable to actions | `audit_logger.py` (principal field) | 03.03.02 | ISO 27001 A.8.15 |
| 3.3.4 | Alert on audit logging process failure | `audit_logger.py` fail-closed | 03.03.04 | ISO 27001 A.8.16 |
| 3.3.8 | Protect audit info from unauthorized mod | `audit_logger.py` (0600 + hash-chained) | 03.03.08 | ISO 27001 A.8.15 |
| 3.3.9 | Limit audit-mgmt to subset of users | `lliam-gov audit` CLI ACL | 03.03.09 | ISO 27001 A.8.5 |
| 3.4.6 | Least functionality | Gateway trim + dashboard hardening | 03.04.06 | ISO 27001 A.8.20–A.8.23 |
| 3.5.10 | No plaintext credentials | `key_manager.py` (keyring-only) | 03.05.10 | ISO 27001 A.5.17 / A.8.5 |
| 3.8.9 | Protect backup CUI confidentiality | `key_manager.py` | 03.08.09 | ISO 27001 A.8.13; ISO 42001 A.4.3 |
| 3.13.1 | Boundary monitoring/control/protection | `egress.py` + gateway trim | 03.13.01 | ISO 27001 A.8.20–A.8.23 |
| 3.13.8 | Cryptographic mechanisms — CUI in transit | `egress.py` (TLS 1.2+) | 03.13.08 | ISO 27001 A.8.24 |
| 3.13.11 | FIPS-validated cryptography for CUI | `runtime_guard.py` FIPS hard gate | 03.13.11 | ISO 27001 A.8.24; FIPS 140-3 |
| 3.13.16 | Protect CUI at rest | `key_manager.py` | 03.13.16 **(Withdrawn — incorporated into 03.13.08)** | ISO 27001 A.8.24; ISO 42001 A.4.3 |
| 3.14.2 | Protection from malicious code | SCA + Katmai EDR | 03.14.02 | ISO 27001 A.8.7 / A.8.8 |
| 3.14.3 | Monitor security alerts/advisories | SCA (quarterly cadence) | 03.14.03 | ISO 27001 A.5.7 |

| A.4.3 | Data resources | `key_manager.py` + `cui.py` | SP 800-171 §3.13.16; ISO 27001 A.8.24 | **Corrected** from A.8.4 |

| A.6.1.2 | Objectives for responsible development of AI systems | This matrix + plan §1 | — | **Corrected** from A.9.2 |
| A.6.2.4 | AI system verification and validation | `selfmod_gate.py` | — | Kept; reinterpreted from "Concepts" |
| A.6.2.6 | AI system operation and monitoring | `selfmod_gate.py` + `audit_logger.py` | — | **Added** in Phase 2 |
| A.6.2.8 | AI system recording of event logs | `audit_logger.py` | ISO 27001 A.8.15; SP 800-171 §3.3.1 | **Added** in Phase 2 |
| A.7.4 | Quality of data for AI systems | `audit_logger.py` + SCA | ISO 27001 A.5.34 | Kept; minor title diff |

## Section: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 — body clauses (6 rows)

Clauses 4, 6, 7.5, 8, 9.1, 10 from plan §3.4. These are management-system requirements, not Annex A controls, but constitute documented information the AIMS expects.

| Clause | Title | Lliam owner | Cross-mapped |
|---|---|---|---|
| 4 | Context of the organization | AIMS scope (Katmai-owned) | — |
| 6 | Planning | Hardening overlay design | ISO 27001 Clause 6 |
| 7.5 | Documented information | `audit_logger.py` + this matrix + NOTICE | ISO 27001 Clause 7.5 |
| 8 | Operation | Hardening overlay live operation | ISO 27001 Clause 8 |
| 9.1 | Monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation | SCA + audit log review | ISO 27001 Clause 9.1 |
| 10 | Improvement | Quarterly improvement cycle | ISO 27001 Clause 10 |

## Section: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 — Annex A controls (23 rows)

These are the controls where Lliam-GOV contributes operating evidence to the Katmai ISMS. ISO/IEC 27001:2022 numbers Annex A as `5.X` / `8.X` (no `A.` prefix in body); the matrix uses the canonical `A.X.Y` form.

| Control | Title | Lliam owner | Cross-mapped |
|---|---|---|---|
| A.5.7 | Threat intelligence | SCA | SP 800-171 §3.14.3 |
| A.5.20 | Addressing information security within supplier agreements | SCA + NOTICE | — |
| A.5.21 | Managing information security in the ICT supply chain | SCA + Hermes quarterly review | — |
| A.5.22 | Monitor/review/change-mgmt of supplier services | Quarterly Hermes CHANGELOG review | — |
| A.5.23 | Information security for use of cloud services | Operator runbook (model-endpoint posture) | — |
| A.5.31 | Legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements | Governance (CMMC/CUI compliance) | — |
| A.5.32 | Intellectual property rights | NOTICE + LICENSE (MIT compliance) | — |
| A.6.3 | Information security awareness, education and training | Operator runbook | — |
| A.8.5 | Secure authentication | `principal.py` + gateway Bearer/HMAC | SP 800-171 §3.1.1 / §3.1.2 |
| A.8.6 | Capacity management | Audit log monthly rotation + capacity check | — |
| A.8.8 | Management of technical vulnerabilities | SCA | SP 800-171 §3.14.2 |
| A.8.10 | Information deletion | `cui.py` on-delete sanitization | — |
| A.8.11 | Data masking | `audit_logger.py` params_hash pattern | — |
| A.8.12 | Data leakage prevention | `egress.py` allowlist + `cui.py` marking | — |
| A.8.15 | Logging | `audit_logger.py` | SP 800-171 §3.3.1 / §3.3.2 / §3.3.8 |
| A.8.16 | Monitoring activities | `audit_logger.py` review cadence | SP 800-171 §3.3.4 |
| A.8.20 | Networks security | `egress.py` + gateway trim | SP 800-171 §3.13.1 |
| A.8.21 | Security of network services | `egress.py` (TLS 1.2+) | — |
| A.8.22 | Segregation of networks | Dashboard loopback-only | — |
| A.8.23 | Web filtering | `egress.py` allowlist | — |
| A.8.24 | Use of cryptography | `key_manager.py` + `runtime_guard.py` FIPS gate | SP 800-171 §3.13.11 / §3.13.16; ISO 42001 A.4.3 |
| A.8.28 | Secure coding | Upstream Hermes practice + PR review | — |
| A.8.30 | Outsourced development | Hermes lineage in NOTICE | — |

## Current state at Phase 2 exit

The CSV's `current_state` column gives the live state. At Phase 2 exit (this commit):

- `SP800-171_3.4.6` (least functionality): `implemented` (Phase 1 gateway trim + dashboard break-glass removed)
- `ISO27001_A.5.32` (intellectual property): `implemented` (Phase 1 NOTICE + LICENSE)
- `ISO27001_A.8.20` (networks security): `scaffolded` (Phase 1 gateway trim is one layer; egress allowlist is Phase 4)
- `ISO27001_A.8.22` (segregation of networks): `implemented` (Phase 1 dashboard loopback-only enforcement)
- `ISO27001_A.8.30` (outsourced development): `implemented` (Phase 1 NOTICE documents the Hermes upstream lineage)
- All other 50 rows: `not_implemented` (will flip during Phases 3–5 per the implementation owner)

## Phase progression (target deltas)

- **Phase 3** lands `key_manager.py` and `audit_logger.py`. Expected `current_state` flips: **~13 rows** to `implemented` / `tested`.
- **Phase 4** lands `principal.py`, `capabilities.py`, `egress.py`, `runtime_guard.py`, `selfmod_gate.py`, `cui.py`. Expected flips: **~30 rows**.
- **Phase 5** generates the AEP exports + SBOM + dependency review evidence. Expected flips: **all rows to `evidence_captured`**.
- **Phase 6** ingests the matrix into the Katmai ISO 42001 NotebookLM and registers Lliam-GOV in the AIMS asset inventory.

## Out-of-band SP 800-171 controls (92 of 110 not Lliam-touching)

The plan §3.3 enumerates **18 of 110** SP 800-171 Rev. 2 requirements as Lliam-touching. The other 92 are organizational controls satisfied by Katmai's existing CMMC L2 posture (endpoint MDM/EDR, identity management, physical security, personnel screening, etc.) and are out-of-scope for this matrix. Phase 6 cross-references this matrix against the Katmai CMMC L2 system security plan to confirm no gap at the boundary.

## Regenerating this matrix

```bash
cd /Volumes/WORKSPACE/1-Projects/lliam-gov
python3 evidence/phase2/build-matrix.py
```

The script runs `pdftotext -layout` against the four source PDFs in `/Volumes/WORKSPACE/3-Resources/`, then regex-extracts each control's verbatim text. CSV is regenerated from scratch each run; this markdown mirror is hand-authored — update it by hand when the corrected mapping changes materially.
